REMODELED KITCHEN WITH GRANITE TOPS, ALL-NEW STAINLESS STEEL APPLIANCES (so new that they’re not yet pictured in the photos), OVERHEAD RANGE HOOD, MICROWAVE OVEN, STOVE, DISHWASHER, REFRIGERATOR with 12 ft of counter space, double bowl stainless steel sink, and ample sturdy and beautiful oak cabinetry with crown molding. title: 1or2 Bedroom, w/Livingroom, Dinning room, Kitchen
description: ++++ Come and enjoy living in a rural setting, surrounded by trees & plants. Live safe and privately in this pleasant apartment. This is a pet friendly house. This apt. is rented as an 'atelier' a place for inspiration from its historic and antique setting. The front apartment is a real antique, with original trim and wide pine board flooring. There are two outside porches one on the front facing west catching the sunsets. and the other on the north side, making it a pleasant place to sit in the cool shade during the summer. There is lawn, trees and gardens surrounding the house. From the front door one enters the front hall and stairway. The stair's railing and spindles are original hand turned cherry. from the hallway one can enter the living room on the left or go straight ahead to go into the dining room. The living room is in the front with 4 large windows bring in lots of light. These windows look out on the porch and beyond. The dining room has a decorative fireplace. There is access to the bathroom, with a tub, on the right. On the left is a conventional kitchen. The bedroom is upstairs, it has a small balcony overlooking the Brooktondale Gorge. There is a walk-in closet and a finished attic space, that can be used for storage, as well. This apt. can be economical with the utilities. There are three zones to control heat usage. The apt. is well insulated. There is insulation in the ceiling and roof. Gas is used for heat and cooking. Electric heat is also available. Internet is provided by Spectrum. This is an efficient apartment. It works very well for an individual or a couple. Public transportation is available in front of the house. There are buses that go to Cornell University and down town Ithaca and up to Ithaca College. The house is located in the quaint Brooktondale hamlet, a comfortable and diverse community 7 miles from Ithaca and Cornell University. Across the street from the house is the Brooktondale gorge. A wonderful and tranquil place to escape summer's heat, one can fish or swim or explore. A short walk down the road from the house is the Brooktondale Community Center where there is a weekly farmer's market were local produce and crafts are sold. A little further on the road is the Brookton Market a very nice place to get coffee and food and choose one of the 20+ craft draft beers. Live entertainment has been there in the past. The market also sells regional and organic produce. There are many hiking trails, near-by, giving easy access to outdoor activities. The Finger Lakes Trail and Shindagin Hollow State Forest are a short drive away. The 'Doll House' as it once was called is over a century old. An antique with cranky aspects about it. These features create a charm that can be enjoyed. Many of the old materials have been preserved old floorboards, trim, windows, doors, hardware. A more rustic and casual look then a modern apartment. Caring for the house is part of the enjoyment of living here in a country house. living in an old country house has certain inconveniences that need to be tolerated. It is not rented as a modern residential apt.. We hope to have friendly relationships with our neighbors to create a more pleasant living environment. A person can live here with reasonable comfort. +++++++
address: 3 white church rd. near valley rd.

title: Great Apartment at Cornell (unit 3) FREE Heat!
description: For a single person, we will let this go for just $1,500/ month!  ($1,700 for 2) available with start in August 2024.  Fantastic Very LARGE one-bedroom apartment with its own balcony porch overlooking a wooded area and large study room in kitchen alcove. Quiet location a few minutes' walk from central Cornell campus and downtown Ithaca. This is a spacious recently painted unit. Laundry facilities in the building, which is a historic Victorian built originally as the ATO fraternity house in 1890, retaining hardwood floors and spacious rooms.  Easy street parking or lot rental is available next door neighbor. Don't let this get away, our units rent out fast and this unit has not been available since 2019!! Recently painted, FREE Heat! Also, water, sewer, and gas utilities are included FREE.  Small electric fee, usually $15 to $20 / month depending on usage. Unit is currently partly semi-furnished, but we have added furnishings available if needed, with a spacious living room, kitchen and bath.   Call show contact info / show contact info for an appointment. Some common questions, and their answers: 1.      Are these apartments in a building complex? -- No, the building is a stand-alone house opposite the road from Cornell University. 2.      How many apartments are there and are they generally occupied by students? -- We have six units in the building, 2 on each floor.  The occupancy varies but is usually graduate MA/MSc/PhD, and professional mostly working at Cornell. 3.      What measures are taken toward personal and property security? -- We suggest that all tenants get renters insurance, that said there have been no robberies in the building and it is a very safe neighborhood in a historic district next to Cornell. 4.      How is the lighting in the building; Lighted entries? Adequate external lighting? -- Yes as required by law, the street is also well-lighted. 5.      Does the property have functioning smoke and CO detectors? -- Yes as required by law, and we ask tenants not to tamper with any of these, but to notify us immediately if they begin to malfunction for any reason. 6.      Does the building have a Certificate of Compliance from the Ithaca City Building Department? -- Yes, it is regularly examined and re-certified. 7.      Is there a bus nearby? -- Yes, the nearest TCAT bus stop is about half a block away, it runs every 10 min daytime during the week, also we are about 15 min walk from the old center of Cornell where the main university library is located. 8.      Floor Plan of the Apartment? -- These are included with each listing on Craig's list or apartments.com, see pictures. 9.      Which utilities are included in the rent?  -- Heat, sewer, water, hot water, and gas for cooking are included in the basic rent, electric and wifi are not included 10.   Is there an adequate supply of hot water and how is the temperature regulated? -- These are from standard hot water tanks, almost double the size needed for demand of this degree, set on a thermostat 11.   Need the apartment to be freshly painted and cleaned before handover?  -- Repainting is done as needed, we ask that tenants NOT paint on their own without consulting us because there are particular kinds and colors of paint to be used. 12.   What is the type of flooring in each room:  hardwood or carpet? -- Most rooms are hardwood, some bedrooms are carpeted. See floor plan for detail. 13.   Doors and windows and mailbox locks?  -- Mailboxes are not locked, but they are in a limited access front hall, windows have locking mechanisms. 14.   Intercom facility? -- There is no intercom installed. 15.   Can you give us a complete list of furniture in each room that you will provide including electrical fixtures?  -- This is as needed and varies greatly from unit to unit.  We do our best to help out incoming tenants from our storage supplies to settle you in quickly. 16.   List of all kitchen utilities and appliances? -- Gas stove and fridge are standard, many units also have microwaves provided, and we can help you out with a variety of smaller kitchen items that you might need from our storage supplies. 17.   Bathroom fittings? -- The usual sink, toilet and tub, we provide a basic shower curtain but you may prefer your own. 18.   How is the apartment maintained, is there a maintenance service contract esp. for the ACs, heaters, kitchen utilities?  What do you do if there is a breakdown etc? -- We have service on call for basic repairs, please let us know promptly if there is a major issue and we will have it attended to as quickly as possible. 19.   Are the beds with mattresses? -- If there is a bed needed there is a mattress provided, you may of course bring your own. 20.   Curtains and blinds? -- Some are provided, again depending on the unit. 21.   Number of closets and storage places? -- Again number and arrangement depend on the unit, but most have at least two closets. 22.   How is the laundry / washers & dryers operated? -- They are coin-op, $3.50 for wash, $1.50 dry.

title: $750 - Room Available in Shared House on 7 acres
description: Hello! We are four people who live on a 7-acre property at the edge of the village of Trumansburg, and we are in search of someone to move into one of the upstairs bedrooms of our 4-bedroom farmhouse. This warm, sunny room is 10’ x 16’ with east and south facing windows, built-in shelves and hardwood flooring. There is a small closet just outside the room, plus a rack you can use inside the room to hang clothes. You would share the upstairs bathroom with two of us. There is also a small cabin that may be available to use as a bedroom instead of the one in the house. This is an ideal place for anyone who enjoys nature, gardening, landscaping, and doing projects on the land. We have some peach trees, hazelnuts, elderberries, currants and lots of wild berries for foraging. There's plenty of space to grow plants of your choice in our large garden, and lots of wood around for building fun structures. We have a cob pizza oven, a big yard for outdoor activities and a clean pond for swimming. There are four of us in the household: Eva (the homeowner) is a vibrant woman in her prime and a keen meditator who loves to garden and walk in nature. Shelly is a heart-centered being who loves cooking nourishing foods. She is interested in alternative medicine, fermenting, and building community. Nina has a deep love of the arts. She writes songs, meditates, and works as a teacher. Kat is a warm, playful and creative being that is into recovery, food rescue, and connection. We like to live with people who practice mindfulness, have an open mind, and are committed to living in community. We value deep listening, honest discussion, and a caring attitude. Each of us has a spiritual focus, and we strive to support each other in our practices and our well-being. The world is so intense these days and living with others who share some basic values around mindfulness, compassion, joyful living and interbeing is a very grounding experience! One of our guiding principles is something Charles Eisenstein wrote about Reverence - carrying the awareness that we are all sacred beings (https://charleseisenstein.org/essays/we-can-do-better-than-this/). It should give you a good idea of what we are striving for in our little community. Currently there's one vegetarian and three omnivores in the household, and we share a meal each month before our house meeting, plus some unplanned gatherings and meals. We have a food fund that everyone contributes to monthly for purchasing shared staple foods, like rice, beans, oils, etc. Sometimes we buy big bags of veggies to share - locally and organically grown. We have a rotating system for how we share housework. In the winter we like to gather around our wood stove, which means there's kindling to be gathered and firewood to cut ourselves or purchase collectively. There's also a far infrared sauna in the house! We have one indoor/outdoor cat, Cleo, who is about 3 years old. She's both cuddly and rambunctious, and has an amazing variety of vocal expressions! We are open to adding another pet to the house, as long as they are a good match. The rent is $750 per month for the room; this includes electric, gas, water and internet. A $500 security deposit is required, along with the first month’s rent, and we use a month-to-month lease. The room is available for a June/July move-in. Makers, musicians, gardeners, builders and fermenters are encouraged to apply! To get the ball rolling, please tell us a little bit about yourself, your interests and your experience living in community with others. We look forward to meeting you :) Peace, Eva, Shelly, Nina and Kat

You’d have your own bedroom, the rest of the space is shared. The bedroom would be unfurnished. The odor free compost toilet does require some maintenance which you will be taught during orientation. The cabin has AC and is heated.  As mentioned, in order to get to the bedroom you must climb up a short ladder to the loft.  The loft has a low ceiling and angled roof, which is shorter on the sides.  My son is 6'3" and he has lived in the tiny house and found it comfortable. The cabin is located adjacent to the Hammond Hill State Forest with 20 miles of trails for cross-country skiing, hiking and biking.  Located just 12 miles from Ithaca, NY home of beautiful Gorges and state parks as well as Cornell University and Ithaca College.  It' is about an hour's drive from Watkins Glen, NY.  The cabin is perfect for those who enjoy a peaceful country setting.  This is an adult space and is strictly limited to not more then two people.  Pets are not permitted.
